prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, in the ninth moon of the Year of Our Lord 2024, the great cloud-temple of "SaaS-Elan" didst report a surge of golden receipts, though no silver coins were seen to pass through the gates of the earthly keep. Lo, the scribes of the digital realm counted not in sheep or oxen, but in "churn" and "monthly recurring value," which some say is the breath of the digital serpent, others the curse of the idle monk. By the power of the encrypted sigil, the company's servers grew fat on data-honey, yet no one could tell if the bees were mortal or enchanted, for they flew not in hives, but in the ethereal realms of the "AWS realm." And thus, the ledger showed a net gain of souls—though none could name a single soul that had been added, nor where they were buried in the vast ether.
